channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Iranians are back online after a monthslong shutdown but face heavy restrictions
item.description: Iranians began to regain internet access after authorities ended a monthslong shutdown. Users said service was slow and spotty in some areas, with apps like YouTube and Instagram heavily restricted.
item.content:encoded:
Iranians began to regain internet access after authorities ended a monthslong shutdown. Users said service was slow and spotty in some areas, with apps like YouTube and Instagram heavily restricted.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Happy, who taught researchers that elephants can recognize themselves, is euthanized
item.description: Happy, a Bronx Zoo elephant who gave researchers new insight into the animal's behavior and became the crux of a closely watched animal rights case, has been euthanized at age 55.
item.content:encoded:
Happy, a Bronx Zoo elephant who gave researchers new insight into the animal's behavior and became the crux of a closely watched animal rights case, has been euthanized at age 55.

(Image credit: Bebeto Matthews)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Ex-CIA official charged with stealing millions of dollars in gold bars
item.description: A former senior CIA official with top secret-level clearance is accused of stealing hundreds of gold bars worth more than $40 million from the federal government and stashing them in his home.
item.content:encoded:
A former senior CIA official with top secret-level clearance is accused of stealing hundreds of gold bars worth more than $40 million from the federal government and stashing them in his home.

(Image credit: Carolyn Kaster)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: DOJ charges Google staffer over Polymarket trades netting $1.2 million
item.description: It's the second known case of the federal government filing criminal charges against someone who allegedly used insider information to make a large profit on a prediction market site.
item.content:encoded:
It's the second known case of the federal government filing criminal charges against someone who allegedly used insider information to make a large profit on a prediction market site.

(Image credit: Josh Edelson)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: NPR's newsroom shrinks through buyouts and layoffs
item.description: At least 18 NPR journalists have accepted buyouts and another 10 have been laid off as the public media network attempts to save money and reorganize the newsroom.
item.content:encoded:
At least 18 NPR journalists have accepted buyouts and another 10 have been laid off as the public media network attempts to save money and reorganize the newsroom.

(Image credit: Wanyu Zhang)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: More people are going hungry now than at the height of the pandemic
item.description: Food insecurity affects more families now than during the coronavirus pandemic, according to a new survey from the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.
item.content:encoded:
Food insecurity affects more families now than during the coronavirus pandemic, according to a new survey from the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.

(Image credit: Mark Felix)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Former Obama adviser reflects on the 'Battle for American Identity'
item.description: Ben Rhodes was a speechwriter and security adviser for President Obama. His book, All We Say, is a collection of 15 speeches — from Ben Franklin to Trump — about what it means to be American.
item.content:encoded: Ben Rhodes was a speechwriter and security adviser for President Obama. His book, All We Say, is a collection of 15 speeches — from Ben Franklin to Trump — about what it means to be American.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Greetings from Cape Verde, where the sounds of samba, jazz and morna fill the air
item.description: Music is interwoven with the sounds of daily life in this West African island nation, which hosted two international music festivals in April and has been named the African Capital of Culture for 2028.
item.content:encoded: Music is interwoven with the sounds of daily life in this West African island nation, which hosted two international music festivals in April and has been named the African Capital of Culture for 2028.
item.dc:creator: Ricci Shryock

item.title: Palestinians use recycling as Israel's restrictions trigger a trash crisis
item.description: Palestinians in the West Bank live amid garbage following Israeli restrictions. Two Palestinian entrepreneurs are trying to make a change.
item.content:encoded:
Palestinians in the West Bank live amid garbage following Israeli restrictions. Two Palestinian entrepreneurs are trying to make a change.

(Image credit: Eleanor Beardsley)
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: Texas primary runoff takeaways. And, DOJ mass-deletes info on Jan. 6 riot cases
item.description: The Texas primary runoffs have now concluded and major November election matchups are set. And Trump's Justice Department has deleted significant information from the Jan. 6 riot cases.
item.content:encoded:
The Texas primary runoffs have now concluded and major November election matchups are set. And Trump's Justice Department has deleted significant information from the Jan. 6 riot cases.

(Image credit: Stewart F. House)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton
